title: Restorative Yeasty Malaysian Chicken Noodles
description: A yeasty, crisp, and comforting Malaysian dish featuring cilantro and cooked on a salt block, perfect for a sick day. This nut-free-friendly recipe is made with binchotan-charcoal for added depth of flavor.
instructions: Preheat the salt block over medium heat for 10 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together curry powder, yeast extract, binchotan-charcoal powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the spice mixture.
Heat the coconut oil on the preheated salt block and cook the chicken until browned and cooked through.
Cook the rice noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
Add the chopped cilantro to the cooked noodles and toss to combine.
Serve the chicken on top of the noodles and garnish with additional cilantro if desired.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
